assertions.reason: The review index and coverage matrix exist, link the full review artifact set, and show coverage for every mandatory review angle required by the validation contract.

evidence.observed: The review artifact map links `architecture-overview.md`, `dependency-map.md`, all eight required flow docs, `ops-deployment-runtime.md`, `test-ci-governance.md`, `static-analysis-triage.md`, `coverage-matrix.md`, `risk-register.md`, and `remediation-backlog.md`.

The file contains `## Mandatory-angle matrix` and lists all required angles, including Cyber security, Data privacy, Protocol / protection handling, Bad-pattern detection, State machines and invariants, DB schema and migrations, External integrations and side effects, API / OpenAPI drift, Performance and scalability risk, Access logging and observability, Business-rule correctness and ERP data integrity, and Incident recovery and resilience.
Read-only validation scripts found no missing required artifact links in the README and no missing mandatory-angle labels in the coverage matrix.

assertions.reason: The central risk register aggregates findings consistently across the completed area reviews with severity, category, evidence, and affected-surface fields.

evidence.observed: The file contains `## Review-finding summary`, `## Review-finding counts by source review`, and per-area sections covering company/tenant, auth, admin, order-to-cash, procure-to-pay, manufacturing/inventory, finance/reporting/audit, orchestrator/background/integration, ops/runtime, test/CI governance, and static-analysis triage.
Each finding table uses the consistent columns `ID`, `Severity`, `Category`, `Affected surface`, `Finding`, `Evidence`, and `Impact`, which satisfies the contract's consistency requirement.
The register cross-links back to the source review documents in section headings and the counts-by-source table, and it also preserves mission-level evidence constraints in a dedicated section.

assertions.reason: The remediation backlog exists and prioritizes follow-up work using severity, exploitability, ERP integrity impact, operational risk, and sequencing rationale.

evidence.observed: The file contains `## Prioritization method`, including the factors Severity, Exploitability, ERP integrity impact, Operational risk, Implementation sequencing, and Cleanup vs immediate-fix split.

The immediate-fix and later-cleanup tables use the columns `Seq`, `Priority`, `Workstream`, `Why now / sequencing rationale`, `Included finding IDs`, and `Primary source docs`, satisfying the contract's backlog-table and sequencing requirements.
The README links `remediation-backlog.md`, and a read-only validation script confirmed the index no longer treats the remediation backlog as a pending artifact.

frictions: Task delegation to `user-testing-flow-validator` failed with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-xhigh`, so this docs-only validation group was executed directly in-session per `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.

Passive runtime setup probes could not connect to `127.0.0.1:8081`, `http://localhost:9090/actuator/health`, or `127.0.0.1:5433` during this round, but the assigned governance-review assertions remained fully testable from `docs/code-review/**`.
